description: FECC (Federation of European Carnival Cities) events

events.title: Rakovica carnival
events.description: Rakovica Carnival remains one of the most beloved and joyful events in this Belgrade municipality. Since its inception in 2013, the carnival has brought together between 35 and 45 local and international carnival groups each year, parading through the streets of Rakovica with music, dance, and extravagant costumes. The event is known for its vibrant atmosphere and its message of unity, artistic expression, and positive energy. Audiences from across Belgrade and Serbia came to enjoy the colours, creativity, and joy that the carnival brings every year.

events.title: Castelnuovo Grand Parade
events.description: The Castelnuovo Carnival Association celebrates 2026 with the 21st Grand Parade of groups and floats. Castelnuovo del Garda is located on the southern shore of Lake Garda in the province of Verona. The official mask representing the area is Duke Gian Galeazzo Visconti and Duchess Caterina, Lords of Milan, who built the current Visconti Tower in Castelnuovo del Garda in 1387. The event is therefore Sunday, April 19, 2026 , with 43 groups and 13 floats from northern Italy and Europe. Gardaland will be present with its mascot Prezzemolo, ready to be photographed with the little ones. Associazione Carneval de Castelnovo Via Milano 5 37014 Castelnuovo del Garda (VR) Italia Tel: +39 349 7433259 Web: http://www.carnevaldecastelnovo.it Screenshot

events.title: San Francisco carnaval
events.description: Carnaval San Francisco will be held on Memorial Day weekend May 23 and 24, 2026. It features a two-day festival of dance, concerts, food and entertainment and the Grand Parade on Sunday, May 24. The parade features approximately 70 groups from many different cultures including Brazil, Trinidad, and Central and South America. There many bands performing at the festival.

events.title: Frog (Žabarski) Carnival, Ivanić-Grad
events.description: International Frog (Žabarski) Carnival in Ivanić-Grad Ivanić-Grad, the pride of Central Croatia, transforms into a vibrant carnival stage for the International Frog (Žabarski) Carnival — a unique celebration of tradition, imagination, rhythm and colour. As one of Croatia’s pioneering continental carnivals, the event brings together hundreds of participants and numerous carnival groups from Croatia and other European countries, including Slovenia, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Greece, Italy and beyond. Organized by the Carnival Association “Žaba” from Ivanić-Grad, in cooperation with the City of Ivanić-Grad and the local Tourist Board, the carnival features a lively parade through the town and group presentations at the Zelenjak Sports Park. With the participation of FECC representatives, the International Frog Carnival further highlights the spirit of friendship, cooperation and shared European carnival heritage. The Frog is always ready to hop into Ivanić-Grad — bringing joy, creativity and carnival energy to Central Croatia. 🐸

events.title: Helsinki Samba Carnaval
events.description: Helsinki Samba Carnaval is one of the most colorful and energetic summer events in Finland, bringing samba rhythms, dance performances, vibrant costumes, and carnival atmosphere to the streets of Helsinki. The event gathers samba schools, performers, and visitors from across Finland and abroad, celebrating cultural diversity, music, and community spirit. 🎭🥁 For more information: 📧 fecc.finland@samba.fi

events.title: Vrnjačka Banja Carnival
events.description: Since the early 1960s, Vrnjačka Banja has carried the spirit of carnival tradition. Revived in 2005 by the Tourist Sports Center, the International Vrnjačka Banja Carnival has grown into one of the region’s most vibrant cultural events. Featuring parades, concerts, children’s programs, and international carnival groups, it attracts thousands of participants and visitors each year. Today, as a proud member of the Federation of European Carnival Cities (FECC), Vrnjačka Banja holds a prominent place on the world carnival map.

events.title: Pag summer carnival
events.description: The Pag Carnival is one of the most cherished traditions of the island’s community — a time for gathering, celebration, and preserving the cultural heritage that “Pažani” proudly uphold. Its history stretches back for centuries, even being mentioned in the Statute of Pag Municipality from 1433. Today, the town celebrates both a Winter Carnival (held since at least 1884 in its current form) and a Summer Carnival (the oldest in Croatia, dating from 1959). Central to the festivities are unique local customs such as Rićavac, Paška robinja, Paško kolo, traditional songs, the “funeral” and burning of Marko, folk costumes, and Paški tanac. Since 1998, the Carnival Association Markova kumpanija — a proud FECC member — has worked to safeguard and promote these traditions, engaging both locals and visitors in the vibrant spirit of Pag. Through parades, concerts, exhibitions, children’s festivals, and cultural exchanges with other carnival cities, the association has brought Pag’s carnival heritage to national and international audiences. Whether in the depths of winter or under the summer sun, the Pag Carnival transforms the town into a stage of colour, music, and joy — an unmissable highlight of Croatia’s carnival calendar. Contact: Karnevalska Udruga Markova Kumpanija: markova.kumpanija@gmail.com TZ Grada Paga : direktor@tzgpag.hr

events.title: Strolls to the Mirror in Martigues, 19th edition
events.description: IN MARTIGUES (The Venice of Provence), Bouches -du-Rhône, France Presenting a collection of more than 200 Venice Carnival costumes, created and worn by more than 110 costumed members of the Association “Les Masqués Venétiens de France” (The Venetian Masked Performers of France). For the 19th year, Martigues – known as “The Venice of Provence” – is transformed for a weekend to introduce you to the very symbol of the Art and Beauty of its big sister, “Venice, the Venetian.” 110 magnificent costumed figures from France and Europe will stroll for your enjoyment through the alleys and gardens, along the quays and squares. Martigues will live to the rhythm of charm and poetry

events.title: Tikveshki Grozdober Kavadarci
events.description: In the heart of North Macedonia’s renowned wine region, Kavadarci bursts into festive life every early September during the Tikveški Grozdober —a carnival that blends tradition, culture, and community flair. Throughout years, the Tikveški Grozdober has maintained its essence—a triumphant kickoff to the grape harvest season, integrating folk dances, musical concerts, exhibitions, seminars, gastronomic delights, and lively carnival parades adorned with local flavor and pride. Contact Information 📍 Location: Kavadarci, North Macedonia 🌐 Website: www.kavadarci.gov.mk 📧 Email: info@kavadarci.gov.mk

events.title: Civijaški Carnival
events.description: Čivijaški Carnival in Šabac is an international event of satire, humour, and carnival spirit. It has been held since 2008 under the patronage of the City of Šabac and organised by the Šabac Tourism Organisation and the “ Čivijaški Carnival” Association. The carnival attracts participants and visitors from various countries, showcasing a vibrant array of floats, costumes, and performances that celebrate creativity and wit. Each year, the event promises a lively atmosphere filled with laughter, music, and the joyous spirit of community. This year, the carnival will be celebrated together with the traditional Čivijada , from September 18 to 20, 2025. Thanks to its reputation and continuity, Šabac hosted the convention of the Federation of European Carnival Cities (FECC) in 2018, further solidifying its place on the European carnival map as a full member. This year marks the carnival’s 18th anniversary, and a large number of carnival groups from both Serbia and abroad are expected to join the festivities.

events.title: Carnival of Vevcani
events.description: The Carnival of Vevcani, older than 1.400 years, is an interesting mix of paganism and modernism. It is held on 13 and 14 January (on the eve of and at the first day of the New Year, according to the old calendar). The main characteristics of the carnival are: archaism, secretiveness and improvisation. According to its specific features, it differs from all other carnivals in the world. The arena of the carnival events is the whole village of Vevcani, which turns in a boundaryless theatre of its kind during the carnival, where each house, each street is a scene onto which disguised people perform their plays like real actors. There are three traditional masks: bridegroom and bride, August the Stupid and musicians. Remaining masks are, in essence, major or minor carnival groups, who, through their costumes, gesticulations and movements usually ridicule and point to phenomena and persons of the social life. The role of August the Stupid is particularly distinct. Young people, full of energy bear this mask and communicate with the audience by peculiar movements and gesticulations, as well as by specific screams. During the carnival, the disguised participants enjoy all possible freedom and passion to “place the world upside down”, freedom in which the creativity of the spirit is expressed, as well as the sense for improvisation, criticism and sarcasm. Officially, women do not participate in the carnival. Yet, they do disguise, but, compared to men, do not take off the masks at the end. In 1993, the Carnival of Vevcani and Vevcani itself, became a member of the World Federation of Carnival Cities, in which Vevcani, despite of the great dissatisfaction extended by Greece, enrolled the Republic of Macedonia under its constitutional name. Closely after that, Vevcani raised the initiative for the national carnival association, covering also Strumica, Prilep and other places endeavoring to revive the carnival tradition. The first President of the Macedonian Carnival Associat

events.title: Kukeri Carnival “Starchevata”
events.description: If you want to feel the magic of the Bulgarian carnival tradition, if you wish to experience the spirit of the ancient local Kukeri tradition “Starchevata,” if you want to discover the color and energy of unique carnival groups from Europe and around the world – come to Razlog! Come to the city of traditions and experience the spirit of Razlog and Bulgaria! Nestled between the majestic Pirin, Rila, and Rhodope Mountains, Razlog has established itself as a modern tourist destination. Here you will find the largest golf course in the Balkans, the modern SPA resort of Banya with its mineral springs, as well as close proximity to Pirin National Park. Luxury hotels, cozy guest houses, and horse-riding centers offer a variety of opportunities for complete relaxation amidst the beauty of nature. Located only 3 km from some of the most preferred ski slopes in Bulgaria, Razlog is a favorite destination during the winter season. The authentic local cuisine and the hospitality of the people make every visit an unforgettable experience. Welcome to the 10th International Kukeri Carnival “Starchevata” 2027! The International Kukeri Carnival “Starchevata” is one of the emblematic winter events in Bulgaria, establishing Razlog as a guardian of authentic Bulgarian traditions and a center of carnival culture. The town has been a member of FECC (Federation of European Carnival Cities) since 1996 and annually welcomes thousands of guests and participants from Bulgaria and abroad. At “Preobrazhenie” Square, Kukeri and carnival groups from Bulgaria and abroad will parade, presenting impressive costumes, masks, customs, and rituals that preserve the spirit of folk traditions. The event combines the magic of Bulgarian folklore, the colorful atmosphere of international carnival traditions, and the unique spirit of the Razlog region. The official opening of the 10th International Kukeri Carnival “Starchevata” 2027 will take place on January 15, 2027, at 5:30 PM with an official ceremony and
organizers: Social Activities, Education, Culture, Sports and Tourism”

events.title: Carnival in Victoria, Gozo
events.description: The Carnival in Victoria, Gozo (Malta) is one of the island’s most vibrant cultural traditions, celebrated every year in the days leading up to Lent. Festivities begin with an opening parade on the Saturday before the official Carnival week , when the capital’s streets burst into life with music, dancers, and colourful costumes. The celebrations continue with lively parades along Republic Street with satirical floats and performances in Independence Square. Beyond Victoria, various villages in Gozo also organize their own Carnival spectacles , featuring dance performances, creative displays, and plenty of festive spirit, making the entire island come alive with joy and colour.
organizers: Cultural Heritage Directorate, Ministry for Gozo and Planning

events.title: Rijeka Carnival
events.description: The Rijeka Carnival, known as the city’s “fifth season,” is a centuries-old tradition held annually from January 17th (St. Anthony’s Day) until the start of Lent. In its modern form, it began in 1982, initiated by three masked groups – the Halubje Bell Ringers, Pehlin’s Party People, and the “No Problem” group – with the support of the Tourist Board. The program features a series of cultural, entertainment, and sporting events, culminating in the Children’s Carnival Parade and the International Carnival Parade. The Children’s Parade brings together thousands of youngsters in colorful costumes and allegorical floats, while the grand finale showcases lavish costumes, elaborate floats, and carnival groups from Rijeka, the surrounding area, and abroad. The Carnival is a symbol of Rijeka’s identity – a blend of tradition, creativity, and community – and one of the city’s most recognizable brands, inviting everyone to experience its famous motto: “Better to die than to surrender!”

events.title: Balinjerada Opatija
events.description: Opatija Carnival – A Tradition That Never Stops Inspiring! When winter grips the coast, Opatija answers with smiles, music, and masks – it’s carnival time! With decades of tradition, the Opatija Carnival transforms the city into a stage of imagination, satire, and shared joy for over a month. Two signature events steal the spotlight: Children’s Carnival Parade (2026 will mark its 28th edition) – Thousands of young masked participants from across the region fill Opatija’s streets with colour, music, and cheer, creating a vibrant celebration of creativity and playfulness. Balinjerada (43rd edition in 2026) – A unique race where participants ride home-made vehicles with ball-bearing wheels down Opatija’s main street. Created in 1983 by musician Duško Jeličić Dule, it’s a mix of tradition, ingenuity, and carnival spirit, with awards for speed, design, and originality. Other highlights include the Masked Klapa Marathon, where vocal groups in costume perform popular songs with humour and carnival flair. Opatija Carnival is more than an event – it’s an experience, a celebration of togetherness, and proof that laughter never goes out of style. Here, masks never hide a smile!

events.title: Losinj carnival
events.description: Lošinj Carnival primary goal is to preserve the island’s cultural heritage and pass it on to younger generations. We achieve this through a series of activities and events that have become a recognizable part of the local community’s offerings during the winter months, outside the tourist season. Through its efforts, the Association promotes the carnival tradition of the island of Lošinj in the media, throughout the Primorje-Gorski Kotar County, across Croatia, and even beyond the country’s borders. The Association begins its activities on the first Saturday after the feast of Saint Anthony the Abbot, known locally as “Antonja.” The carnival season ends with a grand parade on Shrove Tuesday, just before Ash Wednesday. Traditionally, the masqueraders take over the city of Mali Lošinj through the symbolic handover of the city keys, during which carnival participants humorously deliver the “Pust’s Message” to the people and local authorities. In addition to the regular Saturday masked dances and the election of the Queen of the Lošinj Carnival – who represents both the Association and the city at the Queen of the Rijeka Carnival contest – the Association organizes four events unique to our island: ​•​ Mare M onada – a race of non-motorized vessels held in the Augusta Bay, right in the city center. This fun and exciting event brings together brave and creative participants who are not afraid to take a plunge into the winter sea. ​•​ Maškarani Caffè – a morning gathering in the city center, on the terrace of Caffe Bar Moby Dick, featuring coffee (or something stronger), fritule (traditional fried sweets), and carnival music. ​•​ Kroštulijada – a competition in preparing kroštule, a traditional carnival pastry. The event takes place in the town square, where each year we choose the juiciest and tastiest kroštule. ​•​ Pusni Tuesday – the central event of the carnival season, when masqueraders and other participants completely take over the town. Following the grand parade,

events.title: Pag winter carnival
events.description: PAG WINTER CARNIVAL – various events from the first Saturday after the Epiphany until Ash Wednesday: TANCI – masked balls with live music and guest appearances by famous show stars RIČAVCI – organized, singing visits of Pag’s carnivalists homes ZADNJI DANI – the last days – when all mentioned culminates during a three-day carnival-festival on the main city square, starting with a masked parade on Carnival-Sunday and ending on Ash Wednesday with the burning of Marko – the culprit of all troubles. Contact: Karnevalska Udruga Markova Kumpanija: markova.kumpanija@gmail.com TZ Grada Paga : direktor@tzgpag.hr

events.title: The Arlon Carnival
events.description: The Arlon Carnival, held annually in the historic town of Arlon in Belgium, is a vibrant celebration rooted in local tradition and community spirit. Taking place in the period leading up to Lent, the carnival features colorful parades, lively music, traditional costumes, and festive gatherings. A key highlight is the participation of local groups and associations, who bring the streets to life with creative performances and floats. The Arlon Carnival reflects the rich cultural heritage of the region and offers an authentic carnival experience with a warm, welcoming atmosphere.

events.title: Diest Carnival
events.description: The Carnival of Diest, held annually in Belgium, is a traditional and community-driven celebration taking place in the pre-Lenten period. The event features vibrant parades with decorated floats, costumed groups, and lively music throughout the historic town center. Local carnival associations play a key role, creating a festive atmosphere with strong regional identity. The Diest Carnival combines long-standing traditions with a welcoming and family-friendly spirit.

events.title: Pétange Carnival
events.description: The Carnival of Pétange, held annually in Luxembourg, is a lively and family-oriented celebration taking place in the weeks before Lent. Known for its colorful parade, the event features decorated floats, costumed groups, music, and the traditional distribution of sweets to spectators. With strong participation from local associations, the carnival creates a festive and welcoming atmosphere, reflecting the community spirit and cultural traditions of the region.

events.title: Lugagnano carnival
events.description: The Carnival of Lugagnano has legendary origins: it is said that many years ago, a caravan of gypsies stopped in the Lugagnano plain so that women could give birth. They integrated with the locals and settled permanently. This legend is supported by the fact that in 1956 hundreds of gypsies stayed in Lugagnano for 15 days to celebrate the wedding of the daughter of the “Queen of the Gypsies of Europe.” In 1979, thanks to two friends from Lugagnano and the local parish priest, the first parade was held. In 1980, they created the carnival mask Lo Tzigano , and since 1982 a “Tzigano” has been elected each year by the townspeople. Today, Lugagnano’s Carnival is considered one of the best and most popular in the Province of Verona. The next edition will take place March 13–15, 2026 : Friday, March 13: Gala dinner of the associations Saturday, March 14 (afternoon): Children’s costume party Sunday, March 15 (from 2:00 p.m.): Carnival parade Tiziano Zocca President Associazione Carnevale Benefico lo TziganoEmail: info@tzigano.it
